chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types

text: 2671 - وَ رَوَى هِشَامُ بْنُ اَلْحَكَمِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «لاَ بَأْسَ بِالرِّيحِ اَلطَّيِّبَةِ فِيمَا بَيْنَ اَلصَّفَا وَ اَلْمَرْوَةِ مِنْ رِيحِ اَلْعَطَّارِينَ وَ لاَ يُمْسِكُ عَلَى أَنْفِهِ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2671 - Hisham ibn al-Hakam narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"There is no harm in the pleasant fragrances encountered between Safa and Marwah from the perfumers' shops, and he should not cover his nose."
